title: Sequence Properties
description:
Sequence Properties

Sequence Properties and Results

The following list is a summary of properties for the Sequence.  The details for each property are explained in

Property	Description

Name Property	This property can set a particular name that is assigned to sequences.  You cannot create the force guide sequence with the same name.

You can change the name and up to 32 characters can be specified.  English one byte characters and underscore [ _ ] are available.  Also, you cannot use number for initial character.

Description Property	This property can set explanations for sequences.  The explanation can be freely written using up to 255 characters.

MPNumber Property

ResetSensor Property	This property can set whether to reset the force sensor when starting the sequences. When specifying True, the program resets the force sensor and starts the sequence.  When specifying False, the program starts the sequence without resetting the force sensor.

RobotNumber Property	This property can set the number of the robot that uses sequences.

RobotType Property	This is the robot types that use sequences.  This property is automatically set from the robot number specified by RobotNumber.  You cannot change it.

ForceOrient Property	This property can specify the direction of coordinate system that uses the force control functions during the sequence execution.  You can select from Base, Local, or Tool. Normally, specify the coordinate system that the direction you want to press or search is either of X, Y, or Z.  If Base or Local is specified, the pressing or searching direction does not change even if the robot orientation changes.  If Tool is specified, the pressing or searching direction changes since the direction of Tool coordinate system changes when the robot orientation changes.

LogInterval Property

LogMaxTime Property

LogFileEnabled Property	This property can specify whether the force, torque, or the positional orientation of the robot during the sequence execution is saved in files.  When specifying True, value will be displayed on the graph on the monitor and the file is saved simultaneously.  When specifying False, value will be displayed on the graph on the monitor.  However, the file will not be saved.

LogFileAutoName Property	This property can set whether to automatically set the file name that records force, torque, or the positional orientation of the robot during the sequence execution.  When specifying True, the file name is automatically set with the sequence name + start time such as "Sequence name_yyyymmdd_hhmmssfff.csv".  When specifying False, add "csv" to the string in variables which is specified in FileNameVar to make it a file name.

LogFileNameVar Property	This property can specify the file name that records the force, torque, or the positional orientation of the robot during the sequence execution.  It is used when False is specified in LogFileAutoName.  Add "csv" to the string in variables and make it a file name.

LogRobotLocal Property	For the positional orientation of the robot that is recorded during the sequence execution, this property can specify the Local coordinate system as the reference.  The positional orientation of the robot is recorded as that of Tool coordinate system that is seen from the Local coordinate system specified in this property.

RotationCenterTLX Property	When RotationCenterType is Relative, this property sets a distance in X direction to the rotation center of the force control function.

RotationCenterTLY Property	When RotationCenterType is Relative, this property sets a distance in Y direction to the rotation center of the force control function.

RotationCenterTLZ Property	When RotationCenterType is Relative, this property sets a distance in Z direction to the rotation center of the force control function.

StartCheckPoint Property	This property can set the reference point to check the positional orientation and orientation when the sequence starts.  It is used when PosCheckEnabled is True.

StartPointTolLocal Property	To check the positional orientation when the sequence starts, this property can set Local number to specify the available range.  It is used when PosCheckEnabled is True.

StartPointTolRot Property	To check the positional orientation when the sequence starts, this property can specify the range of the rotational direction.  It is used when PosCheckEnabled is True.  If the difference between the current orientation when the program starts the sequence and the orientation (UVW) specified by StartCheckPoint is within the angle of the value specified by this property, the program determines that the conditions are satisfied.  If one or more of X,Y,Z, or Rot is out of the range, the program determines as failure since the conditions of each direction are determined as AND.

StartPointTolX Property	To check the positional orientation when the sequence starts, this property can specify the range of X direction.  It is used when PosCheckEnabled is True.  When the robot is between the position specified by StartCheckPoint and the position specified by this property in X direction of Local coordinate system specified by StartPntTolLocal, the program determines that the conditions are satisfied.  If one or more of X,Y,Z, or Rot is out of the range, the program determines as failure since the conditions of each direction are determined as AND.

StartPointTolY Property	To check the positional orientation when the sequence starts, this property can specify the range of Y direction.  It is used when PosCheckEnabled is True.  When the robot is between the position specified by StartCheckPoint and the position specified by this property in Y direction of Local coordinate system specified by StartPntTolLocal, the program determines that the conditions are satisfied.  If one or more of X,Y,Z, or Rot is out of the range, the program determines as failure since the conditions of each direction are determined as AND.

StartPointTolZ Property	To check the positional orientation when the sequence starts, this property can specify the range of Z direction.  It is used when PosCheckEnabled is True.  When the robot is between the position specified by StartCheckPoint and the position specified by this property in Z direction of Local coordinate system specified by StartPntTolLocal, the program determines that the conditions are satisfied.  If one or more of X,Y,Z, or Rot is out of the range, the program determines as failure since the conditions of each direction are determined as AND.

The following list is a summary of results for the Sequence.  The details for each result are explained in

Result	Description

EndStatus Result	Execution result of a force guide sequence.

EndStatusData Result	Additional information for EndStatus.

Time Result	Execution time for a force guide sequence.

LastExecObject Result	Name of the force guide object that was executed at the end.

EndForces Result	Force and torque at end of a force guide sequence.

PeakForces Result	Returns the peak values of force and torque during execution of a force guide sequence.
